**3. Tables:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Title:** Asphaltenes in Fuel Oil
    *   **Components/Labels:**
        *   Asphaltene Micelles
        *   Very High C/H Nucleus
        *   Maltenes
        *   Same C/H Ratio as Continuous Phase (Maltenes)
        *   Successively Lower C/H Ratio

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Yield of residual fuel oil reduced by more than 40 percent in the past twenty-five years.
*   Sludge size: As large as 120 microns
*   Impurities within asphaltene molecules: vanadium, nickel, iron.

title: improving-heavy-fuel-oil
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Type:** Pie Chart
*   **Title:** Composition of Exhaust Particulates
*   **Segments:**
    *   Water Containing Sulfate: 17.8%
    *   Sulfate: 22.2%
    *   Soot & Organic Compounds: 59.0%
    *   Heavy Metals: 0.9%
*   **Description:** Particulate composition for MAN B&W 7L40/45 engine burning 180 cSt fuel oil

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Title:** Fuel Droplet Combustion Stages
*   **Components and Labels:**
    *   Stage 1: Spray Droplet, 100 Microns, Spray Droplet enters combustion zone.
    *   Stage 2: Vaporization, Vapor Combustion, Rapid Mixing.
    *   Stage 3: Cracking Shells form, Vapor Combustion.
    *   Stage 4: Cenosphere, 20-50 Microns, Sponge-Like Carbon, Slow-Diffusion.
    *   Stage 5: Ash Residue, 2-5 Microns, Combustion Completed.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Fuel Droplet Sizes: 100 Microns, 20-50 Microns, 2-5 Microns
*   Exhaust Particulate Composition Percentages: 17.8% (Water Containing Sulfate), 22.2% (Sulfate), 59.0% (Soot & Organic Compounds), 0.9% (Heavy Metals)
*   Engine Type: MAN B&W 7L40/45
*   Fuel Oil: 180 cSt
